title: Subscription Trap
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you identify subscriptions you're not using regularly?
What systems can you implement to prevent accumulating unused subscriptions?

stages.prompt: You have multiple subscriptions you barely use. What's smarter?

options.label: Keep them all just in case
options.reflection: Keeping unused subscriptions creates ongoing costs that silently drain your money over time without providing value.

options.label: Cancel unused ones
options.reflection: Perfect! Cancelling unused subscriptions frees up money for things you actually value and use regularly.

options.label: Upgrade to premium versions
options.reflection: Upgrading unused subscriptions compounds the problem by increasing costs for services you weren't even using effectively.

options.label: Share accounts with friends to justify cost
options.reflection: Sharing accounts might reduce per-person cost, but you're still paying for services you barely use individually.

stages.prompt: Why do subscription costs feel so small individually?

options.label: They're designed to seem insignificant
options.reflection: Exactly! Subscription companies intentionally price services low to make them feel affordable, but they add up significantly over time.

options.label: They actually are insignificant
options.reflection: While each subscription might seem small, they compound into substantial monthly costs that could be allocated to higher-value expenses.

options.label: The benefits always outweigh the cost
options.reflection: Unused subscriptions provide no benefits while still costing money, making them poor financial choices.

options.label: They're mostly tax deductions
options.reflection: Most personal subscriptions don't qualify as tax deductions, so you're paying the full cost without tax benefits.

stages.prompt: What's the best approach to managing subscriptions?

options.label: Set up automatic renewals for convenience
options.reflection: Automatic renewals make it easy to accumulate unused subscriptions that silently drain your finances without review.

options.label: Pay annually to get discounts
options.reflection: Annual payments lock you into unused services for a full year, making cancellation psychologically harder even when not using the service.

options.label: Only use free trials indefinitely
options.reflection: Constantly switching between free trials is impractical and often doesn't provide the same value as committed services.

options.label: Regularly audit and cancel unused ones
options.reflection: Perfect! Regular audits ensure you're only paying for services you actually use and derive value from.

stages.prompt: How do unused subscriptions affect your finances?

options.label: They improve your credit score
options.reflection: Subscriptions don't directly impact credit scores unless payments are missed, so they don't provide financial benefits beyond the service itself.

options.label: They're investments in future value
options.reflection: Unused subscriptions are expenses, not investments, since you're not deriving any value from services you don't use.

options.label: Small monthly costs silently drain money
options.reflection: Exactly! Over time, unused subscriptions create a significant financial drain that accumulates without providing commensurate value.

options.label: They offer cashback rewards
options.reflection: Most subscriptions don't offer cashback rewards, so unused ones are pure expenses without financial incentives.

stages.prompt: What should you do before subscribing to something new?

options.label: Subscribe impulsively when interested
options.reflection: Impulse subscriptions often join the collection of unused services that silently drain your money without providing value.

options.label: Evaluate if you'll actually use it regularly
options.reflection: Perfect! Assessing actual usage likelihood prevents accumulating more unused subscriptions that drain your finances.

options.label: Collect as many as possible for options
options.reflection: Collecting unused subscriptions creates ongoing costs without benefits, making it a poor financial strategy.

options.label: Ignore the cost, focus on features
options.reflection: Ignoring costs while focusing only on features leads to accumulating expensive unused subscriptions that strain your budget.
